picks.rationale: Both France and Spain boast exceptional defensive records in this World Cup, with Spain keeping six clean sheets prior to their quarter-final and France recording four consecutive clean sheets. Semi-final matches are historically cagey and low-scoring due to the high stakes, further supported by 23 of 28 knockout first halves going under 1.5 goals in this tournament. The market initially favored Under 2.5 at -155, and the current -110 odds offer good value for a game projected to be 1-0 in favor of France.

Spain presents significant value as an underdog, having won three of their last four head-to-head encounters against France, including a 5-4 victory in 2025 and a 2-1 win in 2024. Their defensive solidity in the World Cup, with six clean sheets prior to the quarter-finals, demonstrates their ability to stifle opponents. Furthermore, the line movement from an implied opening of +380 to the current +225 suggests growing market confidence in Spain's chances.

France has been dominant in this World Cup, winning all six of their matches with an impressive +14 goal differential and four consecutive clean sheets in the knockout stages. Historically, France holds a perfect record in World Cup knockout appearances as the listed favorite since 2006. While recent head-to-head favors Spain, France's current tournament form, combined with the confirmed fitness of key player Kylian Mbappe, who has 8 goals in the tournament, makes them a strong contender to win.
